_comment: Ladder run-02 'Laundromat 2am' podcast-skit — the worked example. Copy to config.json and edit. A ~49s two-host (skeptic Brittney + believer Brad) 9:16 fake-podcast skit at a 24hr laundromat set (unrelated to fitness — that's the joke). Pipeline: (1) ElevenLabs with-timestamps VO per line, one voice per host; (2) two base stills (Higgsfield nano_banana_2, mouths closed) + ~10 img2img expression variants anchored on the bases; (3) Veed Fabric 1.0 lipsync per (still, VO) pair @ 720p; (4) yellow karaoke captions from the VO's char-level timestamps, 3-word cap; (5) hard-cut concat + a 2.5s Playwright/PIL brand end card. Master 1080x1920 h264+aac. See scripts/PIPELINE.md for the source scripts.
title: Ladder Podcast Ad run-02 — Laundromat 2am
set_description: 24hr laundromat at 2am. Two hosts at a wedged-in podcast desk between two front-load dryers. Broadcast mic stands, over-ear headphones, fluorescent overhead light, faded WASH DRY FOLD sign behind. Lone sock tumbling in a back dryer. The set is deliberately unrelated to the product — that is the joke.
brand_guardrails: No transformation claims ('lose 20 lbs'). Ladder sells STRUCTURE, not OUTCOME.
No celebrity name-drops outside the actual coach roster.
Plain-spoken voice — 'Get stronger, with a plan' beats hype.
Lime/black palette only when product surfaces appear.
The believer never mocks the user — the villain is the friction, not the buyer.

intonation_legend._note: Written into script.json so ElevenLabs delivers the intended prosody. Applied inline in each scene's `text`.
intonation_legend.—: crisp em-dash pause, sharper than comma (~0.4s)

voices._note: Exactly TWO hosts, one voice each, held across the whole skit. Real ElevenLabs voice_ids. HER = skeptic (validated as the Ramp v1.2 skeptic); HIM = believer (Ramp v1 believer-bro). eleven_multilingual_v2 with-timestamps.

characters._note: Two bases first (nano_banana_2, 9:16, tight bust, mic foreground, headphones, MOUTH CLOSED). Brittney base first, then Brad using Brittney's still as a bg reference for set consistency. Then fan out variants — each img2img on its host's base as the SOLE reference, prompt describes ONLY the pose/expression change so the background is pixel-identical across all cuts.
characters.still_rules: Tight bust framing, broadcast mic foreground, over-ear headphones. MOUTH NEUTRAL/CLOSED (open-mouth still breaks lipsync). Photorealistic, 35mm lens, shallow background blur.
characters.variant_template: Use the reference image as the EXACT base. Keep face, hair, headphones, clothing, background, microphone, and lighting IDENTICAL. Change ONLY: {change} Lips remain closed naturally, no open mouth, no smile showing teeth. Photorealistic, same 35mm lens, same shallow background blur.

bases._note: generate FIRST; sets the laundromat set
generate SECOND with brittney-base.png as a bg reference so the set matches

expression_variants.change: her left eyebrow raised slightly, mildly skeptical look, eyes narrowed just a touch.
expression_variants._note: 'narrowed eyes' NSFW-rejected first pass — if it fails swap to 'curious questioning look, eyes wide open and bright'

expression_variants.change: her shoulders raised slightly in a small shrug, head tilted a few degrees to her left, palms upturned just visible at bottom edge.
her right hand lifted to touch the right headphone cup, fingers visible at side of head, focused listening expression.
a subtle closed-lip half-smile, the corner of her mouth lifted slightly, eyes warm and amused.
a slight lean forward toward the microphone, attentive engaged look, eyes locked on camera.
head turned a few degrees more toward the camera, attentive engaged look, his right hand opening upward in a small explanatory gesture just visible at bottom-right edge.
a subtle confident closed-lip grin starting, corner of mouth lifted slightly, eyes amused, the trace of a gotcha-look.
his right hand raised with three fingers extended as if counting items, hand visible at bottom-right edge, head turned slightly.

his right hand visible holding a smartphone vertically, the phone screen showing a generic dark-blue workout app interface with white text bars (no logos), held just below his chin, raised slightly toward camera.
expression_variants._note: generic app UI only — NO real logos/screens

expression_variants.change: leaning slightly back from the microphone, satisfied confident posture, head tilted back a few degrees, eyes relaxed.

lipsync_engine._note: Each (still, VO) pair -> one clip. Default veed-fal (veed/fabric-1.0) @ 720p, concurrency 4, idempotent. Hedra Character-3 fallback if fal.ai balance exhausted. Empty text-prompt on Veed Fabric — a text prompt interferes with lipsync.

captions._note: Yellow karaoke from the VO's OWN char-level timestamps (scene-NN-<who>.timestamps.json), NOT Whisper. stitch.py offsets each word by cumulative clip start into a global words.json, then make_subtitles.py emits the ASS (--accent-bgr 3BEBFF). 3-word phrase cap, bottom-center. `caption` per scene is the phrase intent; the actual burned text comes from the VO timings.

scenes.caption: what app today
scenes.text: So... what app are we ad-reading today?

scenes.caption: number one app
scenes.text: Strength training app. Number one in the App Store.

scenes.caption: I go to gym
scenes.text: OK but I already go to the gym?

scenes.caption: workout tomorrow?
scenes.text: Cool. What workout are you doing tomorrow?

scenes.caption: I don't know
scenes.text: I DON'T KNOW... I'll figure it out when I get there.
scenes._note: v2 re-render — v1 read too clean for a skeptic

scenes.caption: LADDER fixes that
scenes.text: Right — that's the part LADDER fixes.

scenes.caption: do what it says
scenes.text: Open the app. It tells you what to do. You do it.

scenes.caption: new every day?
scenes.text: Wait — so it's new every day?
scenes._note: v2 re-render — more natural skeptic phrasing

scenes.caption: coach in ear
scenes.text: New every day. Coach in your ear. Timer built in.

scenes.caption: counts your reps
scenes.text: Like a real one. Counts your reps, talks you through it.

end_card._note: Playwright HTML -> PNG (1080x1920) -> 2.5s silent mp4, appended after scene 22 by stitch.py (auto-appends clips/end-card.mp4). NEVER AI-rendered text — the wordmark is the brand's REAL SVG (Ladder wordmark pulled from run-01-app-sizzle). Black bg, lime wordmark center, lime CTA pill, URL at bottom.

assembly._note: stitch.py: walk scenes in order, ffprobe each clip, build a GLOBAL words.json (each word offset by cumulative clip start), emit yellow karaoke ASS via make_subtitles.py, concat all clips (scale/pad 1080x1920, re-encode libx264 -preset veryfast -crf 20), burn the ASS, auto-append clips/end-card.mp4.

music.note: podcast skit is dialogue-carried; the per-line VO is the audio. No music bed (optional low ambience is a taste call, off by default).

captions.note: yellow #FFEB3B karaoke from the VO's own char-level timestamps, 3-word phrase cap, bottom-center

end_card.note: Playwright/PIL end card from the brand's real wordmark SVG, default on
